tool.name: KAT Filter Seq
tool.description: filters sequences based on k-mer content
tool.command:
## Link sequence file to filter with appropriate extension #if $seq.ext in ('fastq.gz', 'fastqsanger.gz'): #set $seq_ext = '.fastq' zcat '$seq' > 'seq${seq_ext}' && #elif $seq.ext in ('fasta.gz',): #set $seq_ext = '.fasta' zcat '$seq' > 'seq${seq_ext}' && #elif $seq.ext in ('fastq', 'fastqsanger'): #set $seq_ext = '.fastq' cp '$seq' 'seq${seq_ext}' && #else: #set $seq_ext = '.fasta' cp '$seq' 'seq${seq_ext}' && #end if
## Link optional second sequence file for paired-end mode #if $paired_end.use_seq2 == 'true': #if $paired_end.seq2.ext in ('fastq.gz', 'fastqsanger.gz'): #set $seq2_ext = '.fastq' zcat '$paired_end.seq2' > 'seq2${seq2_ext}' && #elif $paired_end.seq2.ext in ('fasta.gz',): #set $seq2_ext = '.fasta' zcat '$paired_end.seq2' > 'seq2${seq2_ext}' && #elif $paired_end.seq2.ext in ('fastq', 'fastqsanger'): #set $seq2_ext = '.fastq' cp '$paired_end.seq2' 'seq2${seq2_ext}' && #else: #set $seq2_ext = '.fasta' cp '$paired_end.seq2' 'seq2${seq2_ext}' && #end if #end if
## Link k-mer hash input with appropriate extension #if $kmer_input.ext in ('fastq.gz', 'fastqsanger.gz'): #set $kmer_ext = '.fastq' zcat '$kmer_input' > 'kmer${kmer_ext}' && #elif $kmer_input.ext in ('fasta.gz',): #set $kmer_ext = '.fasta' zcat '$kmer_input' > 'kmer${kmer_ext}' && #elif $kmer_input.ext in ('fastq', 'fastqsanger'): #set $kmer_ext = '.fastq' cp '$kmer_input' 'kmer${kmer_ext}' && #elif $kmer_input.ext == 'jellyfish': #set $kmer_ext = '.jf' cp '$kmer_input' 'kmer${kmer_ext}' && #else: #set $kmer_ext = '.fasta' cp '$kmer_input' 'kmer${kmer_ext}' && #end if
kat filter seq -o kat-filter-seq -t "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" -T '$filter_options.threshold' $filter_options.invert $filter_options.separate -f '$filter_options.frequency' $filter_options.stats $filter_options.verbose $kmer_options.non_canonical -m '$kmer_options.mer_len' -H '$kmer_options.hash_size' --seq 'seq${seq_ext}' #if $paired_end.use_seq2 == 'true': --seq2 'seq2${seq2_ext}' #end if 'kmer${kmer_ext}'

param.name: seq
param.label: Sequence file to filter
param.help: FASTA or FASTQ reads to be filtered against the k-mer hash

param.name: use_seq2
param.label: Filter paired-end reads
param.help: Enable to filter a paired-end read pair. Both files are processed together and kept in sync

param.name: seq2
param.label: Second sequence file (R2)
param.help: Second read file of the paired-end pair

param.name: kmer_input
param.label: K-mer hash input
param.help: Pre-counted Jellyfish hash (.jf) or a FASTA/FASTQ file from which k-mers will be counted on the fly

param.label: K-mer coverage threshold
param.help: Minimum fraction of k-mers in the sequence that must be present in the hash to keep the sequence

param.label: Invert selection
param.help: Output sequences NOT found in the k-mer hash, rather than those found inside

param.label: Separate into two sets
param.help: Partition sequences into two sets: those with k-mers detected and those without. Works in combination with Invert

param.label: Frequency threshold
param.help: Keep a matching sequence only if a random number between 0 and 1 exceeds this value. Default 0.0 keeps all hits. Useful for subsampling

param.label: Emit k-mer statistics
param.help: Output per-sequence statistics on the quantity of k-mers found

tool.help:
**KAT Filter Seq - Sequence Filtering Based on K-mer Content**
KAT (K-mer Analysis Toolkit) Filter Seq retains or removes sequences (FASTA or FASTQ) based on whether those sequences contain k-mers found in a reference k-mer hash.
-----
**What it does**
The tool loads a k-mer hash and filters input sequences depending on whether those sequences contain the target k-mers. A threshold controls what fraction of a sequence's k-mers must be present in the hash before that sequence is retained.
Common use cases:
- Remove contamination by keeping only reads matching a known reference - Isolate reads from a target organism in a mixed-species dataset - Subsample reads based on k-mer presence using the frequency threshold
-----
**Inputs**
- **Sequence file to filter**: FASTA or FASTQ reads (gzip accepted) to be filtered. - **Second sequence file (R2)**: Optional. Enable *Filter paired-end reads* to supply a paired-end mate file. Both files are evaluated together and kept in sync. - **K-mer hash input**: A pre-counted Jellyfish hash (``.jf``) or a FASTA/FASTQ file from which k-mers will be counted on the fly.
-----
**Outputs**
- **Filtered-in sequences**: Sequences that passed the filter (contain sufficient k-mers from the hash). In paired-end mode this becomes R1 and R2 outputs. - **Filtered-out sequences** *(optional)*: Sequences that failed the filter. Only produced when *Separate into two sets* is enabled. In paired-end mode this becomes R1 and R2 outputs. - **K-mer statistics** *(optional)*: Per-sequence statistics on the number of k-mers found. Only produced when *Emit k-mer statistics* is enabled. For paired-end input, each entry represents both reads combined.
-----
**Notes**
- The **threshold** (default 0.1) sets the minimum fraction of a sequence's k-mers that must be present in the hash for the sequence to pass. Increase this to be more stringent. - **Invert** reverses the filter, keeping sequences that do *not* match the hash. - **Separate** produces both the matching and non-matching sets simultaneously, regardless of the invert setting. - When the k-mer hash input is a FASTA/FASTQ file, k-mers are counted on the fly using the configured k-mer length and hash size. - For paired-end reads, both reads in a pair are evaluated together. A pair is kept or discarded as a unit.
